action.reasoning: Observation: all currently saved checkpoints evaluate to near-random on val, yet the framework still records iter-1 best test score 0.3556, so the original successful artifact is no longer recoverable from current files. Analysis: continuing to hunt old checkpoints is unlikely to help. The most plausible path to regain strong performance is to train a robust CLIP fine-tuning baseline with explicit class balancing and worst-region-aware validation, while keeping the pipeline stable and offline-compatible. We have ample time for one moderate run. Decision: implement a stronger but reliable trainer using weighted class loss, optional region-balanced sampling, differential learning rates for head/backbone, cosine schedule with warmup, and checkpoint selection by worst-region accuracy; then run a moderate 5-epoch training, smoke-test on val, and submit the best checkpoint.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 5 --batch_size 96 --lr_backbone 1e-5 --lr_head 1e-4 --dropout 0.2 --label_smoothing 0.1 --use_weighted_sampler --save_path checkpoints/strong_v1.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/strong_v1.pt --eval_split val --batch_size 128 --output /tmp/unused.json
